ECGDex_2026-06-27_equiv.node-export.json.note: ADDED 2026-06-27 (SIGNAL-ADAPTER-PHASE9-REMAINING-NODES — ECGDex leg, node 3 of 4) — ECGDex's FIRST code-gated fixture + the input for the Phase-9 'compute() ≡ committed export' equivalence gate (env.equiv.ecgdex). Produced by running the current ECGDex DSP (the code bundled into ECGDex.html, manifestHash 7c625af51078) headless via ECGDex.compute({text}) on a committed real Polar H10 chest-strap clip uploads/Polar_H10_AAAAAAAA_20260617_010615_ECG_clip.txt (~6 min @130 Hz, 47000 ECG µV samples, first ~6 min of the committed Polar Sensor Logger *_ECG_part01of05.txt — exercises the pure parseECG Phone-timestamp/`timestamp [ms]`-fs path + band-pass → Pan-Tompkins R-peak detection WITHOUT the Web Worker → per-beat SQI → buildNN → HRV → CVHR) → this ganglior.node-export (recording.source 'ecg', startEpochMs 1781658377723, offsetMin null, events 0 — the short clip is not longRec and has no sustained CVHR oscillation train, so it emits 0 events, like the PulseDex/OxyDex/PpgDex equiv cases; event-byte coverage is tracked in the ECGDex follow-ups brief). This IS the app's exportGanglior shape — ecgdex-app.js exportGanglior delegates to the SAME shared ecgBuildNodeExport, so the fixture is byte-identical to ECGDex.html's own export (the headless compute() leaves schema.generated/provenance/kernel as volatile metadata, which the gate's EXCL strips). Fully regenerate-able: re-run ECGDex.compute({text}) on the committed *_ECG.txt clip; the equivalence-gate diff (excludes generated/provenance/kernel/vo2est/karv) reproduces it with 0 differing fields. GATE B asserts the producing bundle's CURRENT manifestHash still equals 7c625af51078 — turns red the moment ECGDex code changes. Produced BY the current gate-clean ECGDex bundle (the Phase-9 re-bundle).

synthetic_ecgdex_golden.node-export.json.note: ADDED 2026-07-12 (CPAP-REAL-CORPUS-2026-07-11-BRIEF §P9, resolving §M5) — a COMMITTED, content-addressed equivalence input for ECGDex. Before P9, every node's equivalence input was a REAL recording, hence gitignored, hence absent on a fresh clone: ALL EIGHT compute()-equivalence legs SKIPPED in CI. The suite's strongest correctness gate — the GATE-C surface that catches a code change which moved a fixture's OUTPUT — therefore ran only on the maintainer's machine, and a regression that moved an export would have gone green through CI. This input is SYNTHETIC (tools/make-synthetic-inputs.mjs): the vendor FORMAT is reproduced exactly, the DATA never is — closed-form, physiologically plausible waveforms containing no recording of any person and no device identifier. Deterministic (no RNG, no Date.now()), so re-running the generator reproduces it byte-for-byte and inputHashes stay stable. It ADDS to the real-recording leg (which still runs locally and exercises genuine vendor quirks); it does not replace it. REGENERATE the input and this golden together whenever the export shape changes.
